title: Salty Sample Derivatization Protocol for GC-MS
author: Emilia Sogin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.nyxdfxn

steps.title: Dry 0
steps.instruction: Dry 0.5 to 1 ml of a salty sample for 6 hr using an eppendorf Concentrator plus SpeedVac at 45 °C in V-AQ mode. Critical step: the drying in the SpeedVac should take no longer than 6h, otherwise the samples could degrade. Samples can be kept in SpeedVac overnight after the run

steps.title: After removal from the SpeedVac, resuspend...
steps.instruction: After removal from the SpeedVac, resuspend the sample in 250 µL of anhydrous toluene

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 37 °C for 30 min and 1350 r.p.m. using a thermomixer

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL of the clear...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL of the clear supernatan into a GC-MS vial insert and place on autosample for GC-MS analysis Critical step: the samples should be run within 1 day after the preparation, otherwise they will undergo degradation and the MeOX will crystalize, making the injection impossible

steps.title: Ultrasonicate the derivatized sample for 10 min
steps.instruction: Ultrasonicate the derivatized sample for 10 min

steps.title: Ultrasonicat the sample for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Ultrasonicat the sample for 10 min at 100% effiency in a sonication bath

steps.title: Remove solvant under a stead flow of N2 gas
steps.instruction: Remove solvant under a stead flow of N2 gas. Improtant note: Prior to inserting needles into tubes, make sure they pre-cleaned with organic solvent. Regulate gas flow so only the surface of the solvent is disturbed. Needles should not touch the solvent. a 3-D printed drying device with attached needles.

steps.title: Samples can be stored at 4°C until derivatization
steps.instruction: Samples can be stored at 4°C until derivatization.

steps.title: Vortex the sample to dissolve salt...
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample to dissolve salt crystals into the pyridine solvent

steps.title: Add 80 µL of Methoxyamine (MeOx;...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL of Methoxyamine (MeOx; 20 mg/1 mL of pyridine) to dried sample. a The salt crystals should dissolve into the pyridine creating a milky white solution as pictured.

steps.title: Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an room temperature bath
steps.instruction: Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an room temperature bath

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 37°C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 37°C for 90 minutes and 1350 r.p.m. using a thermomixer

steps.title: Remove pyridine solvent from sample leaving...
steps.instruction: Remove pyridine solvent from sample leaving behind salt crystals under a stead flow of N2 gas. Use a thermomixer to maintain constant temperature (25°C) and rotational speed (200 r.p.m.).

steps.title: Once sample is dry, add 100...
steps.instruction: Once sample is dry, add 100 μL of N,O-Bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoroacetamide (BSTFA) to salt pellet. Note: Do not leave tubes open for extended periods of time to avoid added moisture from the air

steps.title: Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an...
steps.instruction: Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an ultrasonication bath at room temperature

steps.title: Pellet salt by centrifuging for 2 min at 14,800 rpm
steps.instruction: Pellet salt by centrifuging for 2 min at 14,800 rpm
